page: OVHcloud l4-90 is a GPU-accelerated server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 22 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9454 processor running at 2.75 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 90.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.09 GB per core, alongside a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, equipped with 24 GB of dedicated VRAM. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark evaluations highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core results placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is strong, supported by dedicated cores and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. The system's memory subsystem achieves a top-tier Passmark memory score, although large-block memory bandwidth remains in the average range. For specialized workloads, the NVIDIA L4 GPU delivers top-tier LLM inference speeds, achieving high token-per-second throughput for both prompt processing and text generation on small and medium models.

The l4-90 offers a balanced resource profile for organizations requiring dedicated compute and GPU resources without the overhead of multi-GPU systems. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores, high-speed NVMe storage, and Ada Lovelace GPU architecture makes this server highly efficient for machine learning inference, data science, and graphics rendering. It is particularly suited for deploying medium-sized large language models, running parallelized simulations, and executing cryptographic or vector-heavy compute workloads.
description: OVHcloud l4-90 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 22 dedicated AMD EPYC 9454 physical cores operating at 2.75 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 90.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB of VRAM based on the Ada Lovelace architecture. It delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and exceptional LLM inference speeds, particularly for small and medium model prompt processing and text generation. Network performance is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This hardware configuration provides a highly efficient balance of dedicated compute and graphics acceleration, making it well-suited for machine learning inference, data science pipelines, and parallelized cryptographic workloads.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server featuring NVIDIA L4 acceleration and NVMe storage for efficient machine learning inference and compute workloads.

NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB VRAM on Ada Lovelace architecture
